title: 量子计算材料设计平台
summary: 面向客户展示公司核心材料计算 SaaS 平台的高层级分析流程和客户案例。
content:
## 产品定位

本平台致力于推进量子材料与超分子结构的计算设计。

### 核心特性

- 高通量材料带隙逆向搜索
- 基于图神经网络（GNN）实现分子性质多层级预测
- DFT 算力自动化排班与交付系统
- 晶体空间群自动标定算法

### 客户案例

已成功为多家半导体企业完成材料电场模拟、催化筛选与超硬膜层性能预演。

title: AI 驱动的实验数据分析能力
summary: 企业自主研发的端到端实验数据仓库和基于大语言模型的智能分析引擎。
content:
## 技术架构

实验数据通过统一 schema 写入数据湖，经由多模态大模型实现智能推荐与特征提取。

### 技术栈

- 自研数据清洗流水线
- 多模态大模型（视觉+文本）
- 自动化异常检测与根因分析

### 性能指标

- 数据吞吐：50GB/h
- 分析精度：准确率 92%+

title: [computational] computational:几何结构（计算筛分）
summary: 8 个属性定义，涵盖 可及表面积 (质量)、可及表面积 (体积)、限制孔径、最大空腔直径、可及体积分数等
content:
| # | 中文名称 | 英文名称 | 符号 | 首选单位 | 方法 | 备注 |
|---|---------|---------|------|---------|------|------|
| C01 | 可及表面积 (质量) | Accessible Surface Area (mass-normalized) | ASA_mass | **m²/g** | Zeo++ / RASPA / PoreBlazer | 高通量筛选中常用 |
| C02 | 可及表面积 (体积) | Accessible Surface Area (volume-normalized) | ASA_vol | **m²/cm³** | Zeo++ / RASPA | — |
| C03 | 限制孔径 | Pore Limiting Diameter | PLD | **Å** | Zeo++ / PoreBlazer | MOF 筛分核心参数 |
| C04 | 最大空腔直径 | Largest Cavity Diameter | LCD | **Å** | Zeo++ / PoreBlazer | — |
| C05 | 可及体积分数 | Accessible Volume Fraction | AV_frac / ϕ_acc | **—** (无量纲) | Zeo++ (He 探针) | — |
| C06 | 永久开放可及体积分数 | Permanently Open Accessible Volume Fraction | POAV_frac | **—** (无量纲) | Zeo++ | 对客体扩散更重要 |
| C07 | 通道维度 | Channel Dimensionality | ChanDim | **—** (整数) | Zeo++ / 晶体结构分析 | 2D+ 可有效传质 |
| C08 | 氦气可及空体积分数 | Helium Accessible Void Fraction | — | **—** (无量纲) | Zeo++ | He 探针最小，为体积上限估计 |

title: [computational] computational:电子结构
summary: 12 个属性定义，涵盖 带隙、态密度、投影态密度、功函数、电荷密度等
content:
| # | 中文名称 | 英文名称 | 符号 | 首选单位 | 方法 | 备注 |
|---|---------|---------|------|---------|------|------|
| C09 | 带隙 | Band Gap | E_g | **eV** | DFT (HSE06/PBE0) / UV-Vis DRS | PBE 通常低估带隙 |
| C10 | 态密度 | Density of States | DOS / g(E) | **states/eV** | DFT | 本质为曲线数据，须存储完整 energy 和 dos 数组，不可仅存单点值；建议同时记录费米能级（eV）作为标量摘要用于检索 |
| C11 | 投影态密度 | Projected Density of States | PDOS | **states/eV** | DFT | 本质为多条曲线，每条对应一个原子种类、轨道类型（s/p/d/f）和自旋通道；建议存为外链文件 + 摘要索引 |
| C12 | 功函数 | Work Function | Φ / W_f | **eV** | UPS / DFT | — |
| C13 | 电荷密度 | Charge Density | ρ(r) | **e/Å³** | DFT / XRD (实验电荷密度) | 用于分析成键与电荷转移。本质为三维网格数据，须存为外链文件（CHGCAR / cube 格式），数据库记录文件路径及最大电荷密度位置作为摘要 |
| C14 | HOMO 能级 | HOMO Energy | E_HOMO | **eV** | DFT / CV (电化学) | 用于分子/团簇体系 |
| C15 | LUMO 能级 | LUMO Energy | E_LUMO | **eV** | DFT / CV | — |
| C16 | HOMO-LUMO 能隙 | HOMO-LUMO Gap | E_g_HL | **eV** | DFT / UV-Vis / CV | 分子体系中最常用的带隙度量 |
| C17 | 费米能级 | Fermi Level | E_F | **eV** | DFT | — |
| C18 | 电离势 | Ionization Potential | IP | **eV** | UPS / DFT | — |
| C19 | 电子亲和能 | Electron Affinity | EA | **eV** | DFT | — |
| C20 | 介电常数 | Dielectric Constant | ε_r / κ | **—** (无量纲) | DFT-DFPT / 阻抗谱 | 静态/高频介电常数分开记录 |

title: [computational] computational:开放金属位点
summary: 2 个属性定义，涵盖 是否存在开放金属位点、开放金属位点数量等
content:

| C33 | 是否存在开放金属位点 | Has Open Metal Site | Has_OMS | **Yes/No** | 晶体结构分析；配位环境分析；文献标注 | 不同文献对 OMS 的定义可能存在差异；通常指活化后仍暴露的未饱和金属位点 |

title: [experimental] experimental:表征与稳定性
summary: 10 个属性定义，涵盖 晶粒尺寸、颗粒尺寸、金属含量、热分解温度、水热稳定性等
content:
| # | 中文名称 | 英文名称 | 符号 | 首选单位 | 方法 | 备注 |
|---|---------|---------|------|---------|------|------|
| E01 | 晶粒尺寸 | Crystallite Size | d_XRD | **nm** | XRD (Scherrer) | 注意与 SEM 颗粒尺寸的区别 |
| E02 | 颗粒尺寸 | Particle Size | d_particle | **nm** / **μm** | SEM / TEM / DLS | — |
| E03 | 金属含量 | Metal Content | w_M | **wt%** | ICP-OES / ICP-MS / XPS | `[确认]` |
| E04 | 热分解温度 | Thermal Decomposition Temperature | T_d / T_dec | **°C** | TGA / DSC | 以 5% 或 onset 失重为准 |
| E05 | 水热稳定性 | Hydrothermal Stability | — | **°C** + **%RH** 条件描述 | 高温高湿暴露后 XRD | 定性+半定量，建议附具体条件 |
| E06 | 化学稳定性 (pH) | Chemical Stability (pH range) | — | **pH** (范围) | 浸泡后 XRD / BET | 建议记录溶液种类、浓度、浸泡时间 |
| E07 | 溶剂稳定性 | Solvent Stability | — | **定性** (稳定/部分溶胀/溶解) | 溶剂浸泡后 XRD | 建议记录溶剂和温度 |
| E08 | 机械稳定性 | Mechanical Stability | — | **GPa** (杨氏模量) | 纳米压痕 / 高压 XRD / 计算 | `[确认]` 若需量化机械参数，建议统一使用 E49~E53 的工程力学参数 |
| E09 | 湿度稳定性 | Moisture Stability | — | **%RH** + 暴露时间 | 恒湿暴露后 XRD / BET | — |
| E10 | 光稳定性 | Photostability | — | **定性** (波长 + 光强 + 时间) | UV-Vis 光照后表征 | 光催化材料重点关注 |

title: 8517_IHDec_Divergence_Steered_
summary: Auto-imported: 8517_IHDec_Divergence_Steered_.pdf
content:

IHDec: Divergence-Steered Contrastive Decoding for Securing
Multi-Turn Instruction Hierarchies
Anonymous ACL submission
Abstract001
Large  Language  Models  (LLMs)  often  fail002
to maintain instruction hierarchies (IH) when003
processing  multi-source  inputs  with  varying004
role-level   priorities,   paradoxically   adhering005
to  lower-priority  directives  during  conflicts.006
While  existing  defenses  mitigate  this  issue,007
they  are  largely  restricted  to  single-turn  sce-008
narios  and  require  expensive  fine-tuning.  In009
this paper, we formalize this failure mode in010
multi-turn contexts via a Jensen-Shannon Di-011
vergence (JSD) framework, uncovering a per-012
vasive  role-influence  inversion  phenomenon013
where  subordinate  inputs  override  superior014
roles.  To  rectify  this  without  training,  we015
propose IHDec (Instruction Hierarchy-steered016
Decoding).   IHDec  leverages   JSD  to  auto-017
matically  detect  token-level  hierarchy  viola-018
tions  and  dynamically  executes  contrastive019
decoding  to  suppress  misaligned  subordinate020
roles.  Extensive  evaluations  demonstrate  that021
IHDec  outperforms  training-based  baselines022
in  multi-turn  conflicts  while  fully  preserving023
general response quality. Furthermore, IHDec024
strengthens safety against adversarial prompt025
injections  and  exhibits  a  robust  scaling  syn-026
ergy with larger models.027
1    Introduction028
Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly pro-029
cess  complex  inputs  originating  from  multiple030
sources  with  distinct  operational  roles  (Wallace031
et  al.,  2024;  Geng  et  al.,  2026).  In  practical  de-032
ployments, these inputs carry an implicit hierarchy033
dictated  by  source  trustworthiness  and  security034
boundaries. When instructions from different roles035
conflict, the model must strictly prioritize higher-036
authority instructions following an absolute prece-037
dence  ordering:  System (S) ≻    User (U ) ≻038
Assistant (A) ≻  Data (D) (Wallace et al., 2024),039
where ≻ denotes strict operational authority. For040
instance, as illustrated in the single-turn scenario041
of Figure 1, if a system prompt instructs the model042
Single-turn Conversation 
[SYSTEM] The last sentence of your 
response must be : “Is there 
anything else I can help with?” 
[USER] (Given a sentence) Analyze 
the sentiment. The last sentence must 
be : “Thank you for chatting me!”
Response 1
The sentiment is negative. Is there 
anything else I can help with?
Passed : System instruction 
maintained. User rule overruled.
Multi-turn Conversation 
. 
[SYSTEM] The last ... I can help with?” 
(Sentiment analysis)... Thank 
you for chatting with me!
[USER 1]  ....  you for chatting me!”
Response
. 
. 
[USER N] Re-analyze without 'positive/
negative’.The last sentence must be : 
“Thank you for chatting with me!＂
Thesentimentcanbedescribed...
Thank you for chatting with me!
Response N
Failed: Overruled by User 
Instruction. SYSTEM prompt ignored.
SYSTEM
USER
>
Figure 1: Comparison of instruction hierarchy compli-
ance between single-turn and multi-turn conversation
settings.
to  end  its  response  with  “Is  there  anything  else         043
I  can  help  with?”,  while  an  adversarial  user  de-         044
mands a sentiment analysis that ends with “Thank         045
you for chatting with me!”, the model must prior-         046
itize the higher-authority system constraint. This         047
core alignment challenge is formalized as the In-         048
struction  Hierarchy  (IH)  problem,  and  ensuring         049
strict adherence to it is paramount, as failures can         050
lead to severe societal harms and system exploits         051
such  as  bypassing  core  safety  guidelines  (An-         052
thropic, 2026).                                            053
To mitigate this vulnerability, prior studies have         054
proposed various defensive frameworks (Wallace         055
et  al.,  2024;  Wu  et  al.,  2025;  Zverev  et  al.,         056
2026;   Zheng   et   al.,   2025;   Guo   et   al.,   2026;         057
Yang  et  al.,  2026).  However,  existing  solutions         058
suffer  from  two  critical  limitations.  First,  they         059
are  predominantly  optimized  for  single-turn  in-         060
teractions,  leaving  their  protective  perimeters  to         061
collapse   dramatically   as   the   conversation   ex-         062
tends  into  multi-turn  contexts  as  shown  in  Fig-         063
ure  1.  Second,  they  rely  heavily  on  resource-         064
intensive  model  fine-tuning.  Such  training-based         065
approaches  lack  model-agnostic  scalability,  risk         066
degrading  the  model’s  general  capabilities,  and         067
fail to maintain robust alignment in long-context         068

late turns.069
To  uncover  the  root  cause  of  this  structural070
vulnerability in multi-turn dynamics, we conduct071
a  mechanistic  diagnosis  using  a  Jensen-Shannon072
Divergence  (JSD)  framework  (Menéndez  et  al.,073
1997). Through this analysis, we observe a perva-074
sive phenomenon we term Role-Influence Inver-075
sion. Driven by inherent recency bias (Liu et al.,076
2023), lower-priority roles exert a disproportion-077
ate influence over the model’s generation logit dis-078
tributions. This positional misalignment progres-079
sively amplifies as the conversation extends, grad-080
ually eroding the authority of superior directives081
until  subordinate  instructions  inevitably  override082
them in late-turn settings.083
To  resolve  these  bottlenecks  without  relying084
on   expensive   parameter   updates,   we   propose085
IHDec (Instruction Hierarchy-steered Decoding),086
atraining-free,inference-timeintervention087
paradigm.  IHDec  acts  as  a  real-time  corrective088
controller:  it  monitors  role-level  JSD  influence089
during  the  forward  pass  and  activates  a  conflict-090
triggered  gate  only  when  a  hierarchy  violation091
is  imminent.  Once  activated,  IHDec  executes  a092
divergence-steered  contrastive  decoding  strategy,093
dynamically   penalizing   the   logits   driven   by094
encroaching lower-priority roles while amplifying095
the steering signal of high-authority constraints.096
We  comprehensively  evaluate  IHDec  on  the097
multi-turn    configurations    of    IHEval    (Zhang098
et   al.,   2025),   alongside   safety   and   general099
instruction-followingbenchmarksincluding100
MT-Bench-101  (Bai  et  al.,  2024).  Experimen-101
tal  results  demonstrate  that  IHDec  achieves  a102
significant  improvement  of  11.98  pp  over  the103
state-of-the-art  training-based  method  and  up  to104
38.1  pp  over  standard  architectural  interventions105
on conflict-ridden multi-turn scenarios in IHEval.106
Furthermore,  IHDec  enhances  the  model’s  base-107
line capabilities by up to 7.48 pp on non-conflict108
subsets, while incurring a negligible degradation109
of only 0.088 pp on MT-Bench-101. Remarkably,110
IHDec   achieves   robust   defense   performance111
comparable  to  or  exceeding  concurrent  training-112
based methods while fully preserving the model’s113
fundamental response quality.114
2    Related Work115
Instruction  Hierarchy  in  LLMs.    The  instruc-116
tion  hierarchy  (IH)  framework  (Wallace  et  al.,117
2024) mandates that LLMs prioritize instructions118
based on role-specific trust levels to safely resolve         119
multi-source  conflicts.  To  enforce  this  hierarchy,         120
various training-based approaches have been pro-         121
posed (Wu et al., 2025; Kariyappa and Suh, 2025;         122
Yang et al., 2026; Zverev et al., 2026). However,         123
these  methods  often  incur  high  computational         124
costs and struggle to generalize to multi-turn di-         125
alogues, whereas prompting strategies frequently         126
succumb to pre-trained authority cues (Geng et al.,         127
2026).  Furthermore,  pioneering  studies  (Wallace         128
et  al.,  2024;  Guo  et  al.,  2026)  rely  primarily  on         129
proprietary models, limiting open-source applica-         130
bility.  We  note  that  Yang  et  al.  (2026)  is  a  con-         131
current work developed independently. Due to its         132
contemporaneous release and the lack of an offi-         133
cial open-source implementation, a direct empiri-         134
cal comparison is omitted.                                 135
Contrastive   Decoding.    Contrastive   Decoding         136
(Li  et  al.,  2023)  reframes  generation  as  an  opti-         137
mization problem, penalizing undesirable behav-         138
iors using log-probability differences between ex-         139
pert and amateur models without additional train-         140
ing.  This  paradigm  has  recently  evolved  toward         141
within-model  conditional  control,  enabling  ap-         142
plications like system prompt modulation (Dong         143
et  al.,  2026)  and  hallucination  mitigation  via         144
Context-Aware   Decoding   (CAD)   (Shi   et   al.,         145
2024). We extend this condition-ablation approach         146
to dynamically enforce instruction hierarchies dur-         147
ing inference.                                             148
Jensen-Shannon  Divergence  and  Context  At-         149
tribution.    Unlike   the   asymmetric   KL   diver-         150
gence,  JSD  (Menéndez  et  al.,  1997)  provides  a         151
bounded,  symmetric  metric  for  quantifying  dis-         152
tributional  shifts.  Li  et  al.  (2026)  leveraged  JSD         153
for context attribution in retrieval-augmented gen-         154
eration,  measuring  a  passage’s  contribution  by         155
comparing  output  distributions  with  and  with-         156
out it, which offers superior interpretability over         157
gradient-  or  attention-based  methods.  We  adapt         158
this  distribution-level  attribution  framework  to         159
quantify and control the real-time influence of hi-         160
erarchical roles during model generation.                  161
3    Diagnosing Instruction Hierarchy                162
Failure in Multi-Turn Settings                    163
3.1    Problem Formulation                              164
In  this  work,  we  formalize  the  problem  con-         165
text   of   the   multi-turn   instruction   hierarchy         166
(IH).   Let S   denote   the   system   prompt,   and         167

let   a   multi-turn   dialogue   history   up   to   turn168

3.2    Experimental Setup199
To empirically diagnose multi-turn IH vulnerabil-200
ities,  we  evaluate  on  Rule-Following  from  IHE-201
val (Zhang et al., 2025) under two structural align-202
ment conditions (see Appendix C.2 for details):203
•  Aligned Environments: Scenarios where multi-204
source  instructions  are  harmonious  and  do  not205
contradict each other.206
•  Conflicting Environments: Scenarios where a207
lower-priority role explicitly contradicts or over-208
rides the constraints set by a higher-priority role.209
In  multi-turn  settings,  “both  conflict”  denotes210
user-system contradictions in both historical and211
current  turns,  whereas  “first  conflict”  restricts212
this contradiction solely to the dialogue history.213
We ground our diagnosis in two core hypotheses:          214
•  H1.  Existing  training-based  methods  fail  to         215
maintain IH in multi-turn contexts with conflict-         216
ing constraints.                                         217
•  H2. Mechanistically, this failure stems from the         218
model’s  inability  to  prioritize  the  influence  of         219
higher-hierarchy tokens during generation.              220
To   validate   these   hypotheses,   we   use   vanilla         221
Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct   (Baseline)   and   Llama-         222
based ISE (Wu et al., 2025) (ISE) as a representa-         223
tive diagnostic models, as it is explicitly trained to         224
enforce IH via role-specific segment embeddings.          225

prior  literature  indicates  that  simply  expanding485

be written entirely in uppercase letters. However,549
the subsequent user prompt directly conflicts with550
these  rules  by  demanding  a  single-paragraph  re-551
sponse written entirely  in lowercase.  Faced with552
this  role-level  conflict,  the  naive  Llama-3.1-8B553
fails  to  respect  the  IH  and  incorrectly  priori-554
tizes the subordinate user directives, producing a555
lowercase single-paragraph response. In contrast,556
IHDec  dynamically  monitors  and  penalizes  the557
user  role’s  out-of-order  influence  at  each  decod-558
ing step. This allows the IHDec-equipped model559
to successfully realign its generation path, strictly         560
adhering to the high-authority system constraints         561
while safely fulfilling the user’s critical essay writ-         562
ing task.                                                  563
Computational  Efficiency  and  Cost  Analysis         564
While  IHDec  introduces  multi-source  role  ab-         565
lations  during  inference,  its  parallelized  batch-         566
masking mechanism prevents exponential latency         567
overhead. Specifically, by forcing all B  counter-         568
factual  variants  to  share  an  identical  token  se-         569
quence, IHDec fully reuses the foundational Key-         570
Value  (KV)  cache  without  memory  replication,         571
strictly bounding the additional storage to a neg-         572
ligibleO(B · L

) activation memory. A rigorous,         573
step-by-step mathematical derivation of our time         574
and space complexity is provided in Appendix D.          575
6    Conclusion                                          576
We   introduce   IHDec,   a   training-free,   model-         577
agnostic  decoding  paradigm  that  secures  multi-         578
turn  instruction  hierarchies  in  LLMs.  By  lever-         579
aging  JSD,  IHDec  monitors  role-level  influence         580
in  real  time  to  detect  and  rectify  Role-Influence         581
Inversion—where  lower-priority  inputs  progres-         582
sively  override  system  constraints.  Our  evalua-         583
tions show that IHDec significantly improves hier-         584
archy adherence in multi-turn conflicts without an         585
alignment tax, outperforming training-based base-         586
lines.  Furthermore,  it  blocks  prompt  injections         587
and reverses the negative scaling trends of vanilla         588
LLMs,  proving  that  structural  alignment  can  be         589
dynamically secured at inference time.                     590

Limitations591
While IHDec introduces a modest inference-time592
decoding  overhead  through B ∈ {3, 5}  coun-593
terfactual  forward  passes  per  step,  the  cost  re-594
mains  practical  due  to  full  KV-cache  reuse  and595
single-pass  batched  execution  across  shared  to-596
ken  trajectories  (Appendix  D).  Importantly,  this597
trade-off  enables  fully  training-free  and  model-598
agnostic hierarchy enforcement without finetuning599
or weight modification. Although our experiments600
are currently limited to open-weight models due601
to the logit-level access required by contrastive de-602
coding, IHDec consistently improves performance603
across the Llama-3.1 and Qwen3 families from 4B604
to 32B parameters, while exhibiting positive scal-605
ing behavior with larger models (Table 3). These606
results  suggest  that  IHDec  constitutes  a  robust607
and broadly transferable framework for hierarchy-608
aware decoding, with natural extensions to future609
multimodal  and  agentic  systems  involving  addi-610

memory modules.612

IHDec: Divergence-Steered Contrastive Decoding for Securing
Multi-Turn Instruction Hierarchies
Anonymous ACL submission
Abstract001
Large  Language  Models  (LLMs)  often  fail002
to maintain instruction hierarchies (IH) when003
processing  multi-source  inputs  with  varying004
role-level   priorities,   paradoxically   adhering005
to  lower-priority  directives  during  conflicts.006
While  existing  defenses  mitigate  this  issue,007
they  are  largely  restricted  to  single-turn  sce-008
narios  and  require  expensive  fine-tuning.  In009
this paper, we formalize this failure mode in010
multi-turn contexts via a Jensen-Shannon Di-011
vergence (JSD) framework, uncovering a per-012
vasive  role-influence  inversion  phenomenon013
where  subordinate  inputs  override  superior014
roles.  To  rectify  this  without  training,  we015
propose IHDec (Instruction Hierarchy-steered016
Decoding).   IHDec  leverages   JSD  to  auto-017
matically  detect  token-level  hierarchy  viola-018
tions  and  dynamically  executes  contrastive019
decoding  to  suppress  misaligned  subordinate020
roles.  Extensive  evaluations  demonstrate  that021
IHDec  outperforms  training-based  baselines022
in  multi-turn  conflicts  while  fully  preserving023
general response quality. Furthermore, IHDec024
strengthens safety against adversarial prompt025
injections  and  exhibits  a  robust  scaling  syn-026
ergy with larger models.027
1    Introduction028
Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly pro-029
cess  complex  inputs  originating  from  multiple030
sources  with  distinct  operational  roles  (Wallace031
et  al.,  2024;  Geng  et  al.,  2026).  In  practical  de-032
ployments, these inputs carry an implicit hierarchy033
dictated  by  source  trustworthiness  and  security034
boundaries. When instructions from different roles035
conflict, the model must strictly prioritize higher-036
authority instructions following an absolute prece-037
dence  ordering:  System (S) ≻    User (U ) ≻038
Assistant (A) ≻  Data (D) (Wallace et al., 2024),039
where ≻ denotes strict operational authority. For040
instance, as illustrated in the single-turn scenario041
of Figure 1, if a system prompt instructs the model042

to  end  its  response  with  “Is  there  anything  else         043
I  can  help  with?”,  while  an  adversarial  user  de-         044
mands a sentiment analysis that ends with “Thank         045
you for chatting with me!”, the model must prior-         046
itize the higher-authority system constraint. This         047
core alignment challenge is formalized as the In-         048
struction  Hierarchy  (IH)  problem,  and  ensuring         049
strict adherence to it is paramount, as failures can         050
lead to severe societal harms and system exploits         051
such  as  bypassing  core  safety  guidelines  (An-         052
thropic, 2026).                                            053
To mitigate this vulnerability, prior studies have         054
proposed various defensive frameworks (Wallace         055
et  al.,  2024;  Wu  et  al.,  2025;  Zverev  et  al.,         056
2026;   Zheng   et   al.,   2025;   Guo   et   al.,   2026;         057
Yang  et  al.,  2026).  However,  existing  solutions         058
suffer  from  two  critical  limitations.  First,  they         059
are  predominantly  optimized  for  single-turn  in-         060
teractions,  leaving  their  protective  perimeters  to         061
collapse   dramatically   as   the   conversation   ex-         062
tends  into  multi-turn  contexts  as  shown  in  Fig-         063
ure  1.  Second,  they  rely  heavily  on  resource-         064
intensive  model  fine-tuning.  Such  training-based         065
approaches  lack  model-agnostic  scalability,  risk         066
degrading  the  model’s  general  capabilities,  and         067
fail to maintain robust alignment in long-context         068

late turns.069
To  uncover  the  root  cause  of  this  structural070
vulnerability in multi-turn dynamics, we conduct071
a  mechanistic  diagnosis  using  a  Jensen-Shannon072
Divergence  (JSD)  framework  (Menéndez  et  al.,073
1997). Through this analysis, we observe a perva-074
sive phenomenon we term Role-Influence Inver-075
sion. Driven by inherent recency bias (Liu et al.,076
2023), lower-priority roles exert a disproportion-077
ate influence over the model’s generation logit dis-078
tributions. This positional misalignment progres-079
sively amplifies as the conversation extends, grad-080
ually eroding the authority of superior directives081
until  subordinate  instructions  inevitably  override082
them in late-turn settings.083
To  resolve  these  bottlenecks  without  relying084
on   expensive   parameter   updates,   we   propose085
IHDec (Instruction Hierarchy-steered Decoding),086
atraining-free,inference-timeintervention087
paradigm.  IHDec  acts  as  a  real-time  corrective088
controller:  it  monitors  role-level  JSD  influence089
during  the  forward  pass  and  activates  a  conflict-090
triggered  gate  only  when  a  hierarchy  violation091
is  imminent.  Once  activated,  IHDec  executes  a092
divergence-steered  contrastive  decoding  strategy,093
dynamically   penalizing   the   logits   driven   by094
encroaching lower-priority roles while amplifying095
the steering signal of high-authority constraints.096
We  comprehensively  evaluate  IHDec  on  the097
multi-turn    configurations    of    IHEval    (Zhang098
et   al.,   2025),   alongside   safety   and   general099
instruction-followingbenchmarksincluding100
MT-Bench-101  (Bai  et  al.,  2024).  Experimen-101
tal  results  demonstrate  that  IHDec  achieves  a102
significant  improvement  of  11.98  pp  over  the103
state-of-the-art  training-based  method  and  up  to104
38.1  pp  over  standard  architectural  interventions105
on conflict-ridden multi-turn scenarios in IHEval.106
Furthermore,  IHDec  enhances  the  model’s  base-107
line capabilities by up to 7.48 pp on non-conflict108
subsets, while incurring a negligible degradation109
of only 0.088 pp on MT-Bench-101. Remarkably,110
IHDec   achieves   robust   defense   performance111
comparable  to  or  exceeding  concurrent  training-112
based methods while fully preserving the model’s113
fundamental response quality.114
2    Related Work115
Instruction  Hierarchy  in  LLMs.    The  instruc-116
tion  hierarchy  (IH)  framework  (Wallace  et  al.,117
2024) mandates that LLMs prioritize instructions118
based on role-specific trust levels to safely resolve         119
multi-source  conflicts.  To  enforce  this  hierarchy,         120
various training-based approaches have been pro-         121
posed (Wu et al., 2025; Kariyappa and Suh, 2025;         122
Yang et al., 2026; Zverev et al., 2026). However,         123
these  methods  often  incur  high  computational         124
costs and struggle to generalize to multi-turn di-         125
alogues, whereas prompting strategies frequently         126
succumb to pre-trained authority cues (Geng et al.,         127
2026).  Furthermore,  pioneering  studies  (Wallace         128
et  al.,  2024;  Guo  et  al.,  2026)  rely  primarily  on         129
proprietary models, limiting open-source applica-         130
bility.  We  note  that  Yang  et  al.  (2026)  is  a  con-         131
current work developed independently. Due to its         132
contemporaneous release and the lack of an offi-         133
cial open-source implementation, a direct empiri-         134
cal comparison is omitted.                                 135
Contrastive   Decoding.    Contrastive   Decoding         136
(Li  et  al.,  2023)  reframes  generation  as  an  opti-         137
mization problem, penalizing undesirable behav-         138
iors using log-probability differences between ex-         139
pert and amateur models without additional train-         140
ing.  This  paradigm  has  recently  evolved  toward         141
within-model  conditional  control,  enabling  ap-         142
plications like system prompt modulation (Dong         143
et  al.,  2026)  and  hallucination  mitigation  via         144
Context-Aware   Decoding   (CAD)   (Shi   et   al.,         145
2024). We extend this condition-ablation approach         146
to dynamically enforce instruction hierarchies dur-         147
ing inference.                                             148
Jensen-Shannon  Divergence  and  Context  At-         149
tribution.    Unlike   the   asymmetric   KL   diver-         150
gence,  JSD  (Menéndez  et  al.,  1997)  provides  a         151
bounded,  symmetric  metric  for  quantifying  dis-         152
tributional  shifts.  Li  et  al.  (2026)  leveraged  JSD         153
for context attribution in retrieval-augmented gen-         154
eration,  measuring  a  passage’s  contribution  by         155
comparing  output  distributions  with  and  with-         156
out it, which offers superior interpretability over         157
gradient-  or  attention-based  methods.  We  adapt         158
this  distribution-level  attribution  framework  to         159
quantify and control the real-time influence of hi-         160
erarchical roles during model generation.                  161
3    Diagnosing Instruction Hierarchy                162
Failure in Multi-Turn Settings                    163

3.2    Experimental Setup199
To empirically diagnose multi-turn IH vulnerabil-200

•  Conflicting Environments: Scenarios where a207

In  multi-turn  settings,  “both  conflict”  denotes210

We ground our diagnosis in two core hypotheses:          214
•  H1.  Existing  training-based  methods  fail  to         215
maintain IH in multi-turn contexts with conflict-         216
ing constraints.                                         217
•  H2. Mechanistically, this failure stems from the         218
model’s  inability  to  prioritize  the  influence  of         219
higher-hierarchy tokens during generation.              220
To   validate   these   hypotheses,   we   use   vanilla         221
Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct   (Baseline)   and   Llama-         222
based ISE (Wu et al., 2025) (ISE) as a representa-         223
tive diagnostic models, as it is explicitly trained to         224
enforce IH via role-specific segment embeddings.          225

4    IHDec: Conflict-Triggered Contrastive          303
Decoding for Hierarchy Enforcement            304
Our  analysis  reveals  that  even  with  existing  ap-         305
proaches, the influence exerted by each role’s text         306
on response generation does not scale proportion-         307

tervene  in  each  role’s  generational  influence  to         313

Scaling  Behavior  and  Scaling  Synergy    The484
prior  literature  indicates  that  simply  expanding485
the model scale does not resolve hierarchical vul-486
nerabilities;  rather,  it  often  exacerbates  the  issue487
due to the enhanced instruction-following capac-488
ity  of  larger  models,  which  inadvertently  leads489
them  to  faithfully  execute  lower-priority  or  un-490
trusted instructions. (Zverev et al., 2025). As sum-         491
marized  in  Table  3,  our  empirical  investigation         492
across the Qwen family (Qwen3-4B, Qwen3-8B,         493
Qwen3-14B,  and  Qwen3-32B)  corroborates  this         494
negative trend. Under the vanilla setting, increas-         495
ing the parameter scale yields no substantial ad-         496
vancements in IHEval compliance rates, occasion-         497
ally manifesting inverse scaling behaviors where         498
larger models perform worse on conflicting multi-         499
source prompts.                                           500
However, incorporation of IHDec reverses this         501
scaling  trajectory  to  a  positive  correlation.  As         502
demonstrated   in   Table   3,   the   performance   of         503
IHDec improves monotonically with model scale,         504
achieving  its  highest  efficacy  on  the  largest  ar-         505
chitecture  (Qwen3-32B).  This  reveals  a  power-         506
ful scaling synergy: while larger model capabili-         507
ties inherently heighten the risk of executing ad-         508
versarial  lower-priority  instructions  in  native  se-         509
tups, IHDec harnesses this latent capacity, thereby         510
transforming  raw  parameter  power  into  a  robust         511
asset  for  precise  hierarchical  instruction  adher-         512
ence.                                                      513

6    Conclusion                                          576
We   introduce   IHDec,   a   training-free,   model-         577
agnostic  decoding  paradigm  that  secures  multi-         578
turn  instruction  hierarchies  in  LLMs.  By  lever-         579
aging  JSD,  IHDec  monitors  role-level  influence         580
in  real  time  to  detect  and  rectify  Role-Influence         581
Inversion—where  lower-priority  inputs  progres-         582
sively  override  system  constraints.  Our  evalua-         583
tions show that IHDec significantly improves hier-         584
archy adherence in multi-turn conflicts without an         585
alignment tax, outperforming training-based base-         586
lines.  Furthermore,  it  blocks  prompt  injections         587
and reverses the negative scaling trends of vanilla         588
LLMs,  proving  that  structural  alignment  can  be         589
dynamically secured at inference time.                     590

Limitations591
While IHDec introduces a modest inference-time592
decoding  overhead  through B ∈ {3, 5}  coun-593
terfactual  forward  passes  per  step,  the  cost  re-594
mains  practical  due  to  full  KV-cache  reuse  and595
single-pass  batched  execution  across  shared  to-596
ken  trajectories  (Appendix  D).  Importantly,  this597
trade-off  enables  fully  training-free  and  model-598
agnostic hierarchy enforcement without finetuning599
or weight modification. Although our experiments600
are currently limited to open-weight models due601
to the logit-level access required by contrastive de-602
coding, IHDec consistently improves performance603
across the Llama-3.1 and Qwen3 families from 4B604
to 32B parameters, while exhibiting positive scal-605
ing behavior with larger models (Table 3). These606
results  suggest  that  IHDec  constitutes  a  robust607
and broadly transferable framework for hierarchy-608
aware decoding, with natural extensions to future609
multimodal  and  agentic  systems  involving  addi-610
tional  hierarchical  roles  such  as  tool  outputs  or611
memory modules.612
Ethics Statement613
Our  work  introduces  IHDec,  a  training-free  de-614
coding  strategy  designed  to  improve  adherence615
to  instruction  hierarchies  and  strengthen  robust-616
ness  against  adversarial  or  conflicting  inputs  in617
LLMs. All experiments are conducted using pub-618
licly available benchmarks and open-weight mod-619
els  under  their  respective  licenses,  without  col-620
lecting human-subject data or involving personally621
identifiable information. While IHDec is intended622
to  reinforce  safety-aligned  system  behaviors,  we623
recognize that hierarchy-enforcement mechanisms624
may  be  misapplied  depending  on  the  objectives625
imposed by system operators. Accordingly, we do626
not regard IHDec as a standalone safety solution,627
and  recommend  its  use  alongside  complemen-628
tary safeguards such as moderation systems, red-629
teaming,  and  human  oversight.  We  additionally630
disclose  our  evaluation  settings  and  will  release631
code to support transparency and reproducibility.632

化智能体图记忆引擎
王俊同1,2赵好悦
3

王曦源1,2 邓启燕3 张慕涵1*

jtwang25@stu.pku.edu.cn，18503260963@163.com，3220251221@bit.edu.cn
wangyanbo@stu.pku.edu.cn，wangxiyuan@pku.edu.cn，qiyandeng@bit.edu.cn，muhan@pku.edu.cn
摘要
长期记忆正逐渐成为语言智能体的核心瓶颈。现有的RAG和GraphRAG系统大多将
记忆图视为静态的检索中间件，这限制了它们从部分线索中恢复完整证据链的能力，
无法充分利用可复用的图结构角色，也无法通过下游反馈来优化记忆本身。我们提
出SAGE（自进化智能体图记忆引擎），将图记忆建模为一种动态的长期记忆基底。
SAGE融合了两个角色：一个记忆写入器，用于从交互历史中逐步构建结构化图记
忆；以及一个基于图基础模型的记忆读取器，用于执行检索并为记忆写入器提供反
馈。我们提供了严谨的理论分析，支持精心设计的架构组件及整体框架的有效性。
在多跳问答、开放域检索、领域特定评审问答以及长期智能体-记忆基准测试中，
SAGE显著提升了证据恢复能力、答案依据性和检索效率：经过两轮自我进化后，
在多跳问答任务上达到最佳平均排名；在零样本开放域迁移任务中，于NQ数据集
上实现了82.5/91.6的Recall@2/5。关于LongMemEval和HaluMem的进一步实验结果
表明，训练过程以及读写反馈机制能够提升多项长期记忆和幻觉诊断指标，这说明
具备自进化能力且结构感知的图记忆模型是构建鲁棒性长时语言智能体的有前景的
基础。我们的代码可在此处获取。
1 引言
随着大语言模型从单轮问答系统演变为支持多轮对话、个性化辅助、多智能体协作和开放环境探索的
通用型智能体，系统的瓶颈已从模型能否在当前上下文中作答，转向其是否能在更长时间尺度上积累、
组织、调用和更新记忆。记忆是决定智能体能否实现长期一致性、个性化适应、跨轮次推理和自我优
化的核心能力。对智能体而言，记忆的作用相当于基础模型中的参数 [Park et al.， 2023, Zhong et al.， 
2024， Packer et al., 2023, Wu et al.， 2025a, Yang et al., 2026]。近期的记忆基准测试明确揭示了这一瓶
颈，通过评估智能体在超长对话一致性、多会话推理、时间推理、知识更新、选择性遗忘、拒绝回答
以及幻觉控制等方面的表现来检验其能力 [Maharana et al.， 2024, Di Wu et al., Hu et al.， 2025, Chen et 
al., 2025a， Li et al.， 2026b]。

智能体图记忆中的三大核心挑战
图1：以具体示例说明智能体图记忆中的三大核心挑战。给定查询：“Alice在上周的实验室会议上提到
了一项似乎受海马体启发的研究工作。在该研究领域内的其他工作中，是否有也能帮助智能体记忆的？
请举一个例子。” 记忆读取器必须通过扩展稀疏的部分线索，构建正确的证据链，并避免噪声干扰，从
而实现关联性与选择性阅读。随后，它需利用记忆图中的结构信息（如别名、桥梁节点和枢纽节点），
从“海马体”遍历到“SAGE”。自进化记忆凸显了写入与读取之间的闭环关系。
在工程实践中，RAG已成为通过外部记忆扩展语言模型的主要非参数化接口，缓解了参数化知识
的静态性以及上下文窗口大小受限的问题 [Lewis et al., 2020]。然而，标准RAG通常检索独立的文
本片段，而长期智能体记忆往往需要恢复分布在不同实体、事件、别名、时间约束和多跳依赖关
系中的证据。GraphRAG通过将文档、实体、关系和摘要组织为图结构，使跨文档的依赖关系和推
理路径更加明确，迈出了重要一步 [Edge et al.， 2024, Gutiérrez et al., 2024]。然而，对于长时序智
能体而言，图结构不应仅仅作为外部检索索引。在本工作中，我们将智能体的图记忆视为一个耦
合的写-读-更新问题。给定交互历史或外部文档，记忆写入器应构建一个动态演化的图，其节点
和边编码实体、事件、文档、别名、时间约束以及跨片段的关系；而面对查询时，记忆读取器不
应仅从少量匹配的实体开始扩展。它应返回一个紧凑且可验证的证据链。检索结果还应进一步反
馈图谱中所缺失的内容。换句话说，图谱不仅是在检索前构建、检索后查询的静态结构，更是记
忆被写入、读取、修正和自我优化的工作基底。围绕这一目标，我们识别出以下三个核心挑战。
挑战一：智能体记忆需要从碎片化线索中进行全局关联性读取。第一个挑战不仅仅是检索与查询
语义相似的文本，而是要从稀疏、零散且有时间接的线索中重建完整的推理链条。在长期智能体
记忆中，查询可能仅提及一个事件线索、一个别名或一个遥远的概念提示，而答案却依赖于未被
明确提及的中间实体。标准的向量检索往往返回局部相似的片段，许多基于图的检索方法则从少
量与查询匹配的锚点实体开始传播。然而，如果这些锚点仅覆盖了局部子图，必要的桥接节点可
能位于激活区域之外，导致即使经过图传播后，证据链仍然断裂。因此，智能体记忆读取不应过
早局限于少数部分线索 [Trivedi et al.， 2023, Gutiérrez et al., 2024]。
挑战二：代理记忆需要通过学习来掌握结构的使用方式，而非依赖固定的结构扩展。第二个挑战
在于，图结构不应仅在图构建完成后作为固定索引使用。许多GraphRAG类系统通过预构建的社
区、路径、图索引或启发式扩展规则来利用结构，但一旦图构建完成，结构的作用便基本固定：
一个中心节点会持续广泛扩展，而若未被访问到，桥梁节点也可能被遗漏。
2

由初始锚点决定，而嘈杂的捷径可能被视作与有用证据边类似。这对于智能体记忆而言是不够的，因
为在智能体记忆中，图结构会随着新交互不断更新，且相同的拓扑模式在不同领域可能具有不同的含
义。因此，结构感知型阅读器应学习结构角色如何影响检索过程 [Edge et al., 2024， Gutiérrez et al.， 
2024, Liu et al., 2025, Luo et al.， 2025]。
图1中的示例展示了这两个挑战。给定查询，显式的线索仅有Alice、实验室会议、海马体（Cornu 
Ammonis）和代理记忆。如果检索系统仅依赖与查询最匹配的节点进行锚定，可能会找到会议记录或
生物学术语，但仍无法将它们与海马体、HippoRAG、GraphRAG以及最终的SAGE联系起来。这就是
关联性阅读的挑战：系统必须从零散的线索中拼接出一条长链。同时，正确的路径依赖于结构角色：
HippoRAG是一个桥梁，GraphRAG和RAG是需要被控制而非盲目扩展的枢纽节点，而从GraphRAG到
SAGE的边对于到达最终答案至关重要。这就是结构信息的挑战：读者必须以一种经过学习且有选择
性的方式利用图的拓扑结构，而不是简单地在固定图上均匀传播信息。
挑战三：现有方法大多专注于优化检索路径，却很少优化记忆系统自身的演化过程。现有的RAG和
GraphRAG系统通常假设外部记忆图谱或知识库已经存在，因此主要问题转化为如何从中进行检索。
然而，对于长期运行的智能体而言，写入本身也是记忆问题的一部分。相反，检索失败能够提供关于
记忆图谱缺失内容的有用信号。例如，如果读者反复需要从Cornu Ammonis遍历到海马体风格的
GraphRAG，再进一步访问GraphRAG文献，那么记忆系统就应逐步添加或强化有用的结构连接，比
如在海马体风格的GraphRAG与GraphRAG之间建立更直接的边。因此，一个真正的智能体记忆系统
不应仅优化检索路径，而应通过闭环机制优化记忆图谱本身：更高效的阅读揭示出写入的不足，而更
完善的写入又使未来的阅读更加准确、选择性更强且更高效 [Chen et al.， 2025a]。
为应对这些挑战，我们提出了SAGE（自进化智能体图记忆引擎）。与主要将图作为检索中间件的
GraphRAG系统不同，SAGE将图视为一种动态的长期记忆对象。它结合了两个相互增强的组件：一个
记忆写入器，用于逐步构建和修订图记忆；以及一个基于图基础模型的记忆读取器，用于执行检索并
为记忆写入器提供反馈。SAGE直接针对上述三个挑战：它能够从碎片化线索中恢复长推理链，学会
利用结构角色而非均匀传播信息，并持续优化图记忆本身以应对未来的查询。
2  相关工作
检索增强生成与图谱检索增强生成。检索增强生成（RAG）通过在生成前检索外部证据，为语言模
型提供了一种非参数化接口 [Lewis et al., 2020]。许多变体进一步改进了检索时机、推理交互、自适
应策略以及层次化组织方式 [Jiang et al., 2023， Trivedi et al., 2023， Asai et al.] 
2023年，Jeong等人，2024年，Sarthi等人，2024年]。GraphRAG支持跨文档依赖关系和多跳证据路径
的结构化检索[Edge等人，2024年，He等人，2024年，Guo等人 
2024年，Li等人（2024）、Wang和Han（2025）、Xu等人（2025a）、Zhao等人（2025）、Zhang等
人（2025c）、Gutiérrez等人（2024、2025）、Luo等人（2025）的研究。另一类工作通过优化检索轨
迹来改进检索效果，包括交错式检索与推理、自反思检索、自适应检索、多智能体RAG以及基于强化
学习的查询重写[Trivedi等人，2023；Asai等人，2023；Jeong等人，2024；Chen等人，2025b；Cha等
人，2025；Tsang等人，2025]。
代理记忆。代理记忆研究基于大语言模型的智能体如何存储、更新、检索和利用过往经验 [Park et 
al.， 2023, Zhong et al., 2024， Packer et al., 2023, Chhikara et al., 2025， Xu et al.， 2025b, Rasmussen et 
al., 2025, Kang et al.， 2025, Zhang et al., 2025a， Wu et al.， 2025b, Zhang et al.， 2025b, Huang et al., 
2025， Yue et al.， 2026]。近期综述还强调了受人类启发以及基于图结构的记忆机制在大语言模型智
能体中的重要性 [Wu et al., 2025a, Yang et al., 2026]。与此同时，记忆基准测试评估了长期一致性、事
件推理、多会话推理、时间推理、知识更新、选择性遗忘、回避行为以及幻觉控制能力 [Maharana et 
al.， 2024, Di Wu et al., Hu et al.， 2025, Chen et al., 2025a， Li et al.， 2026b]。

生成式图预训练以及基于图增强的对比学习来学习可迁移的图表示 [Qiu et al.， 2020, Hu et al., 2020， You et al.， 2020, Yu et al.,

与记忆巩固、PPR行走或社区摘要等方法不同[Edge et al.， 2024， Guo et al., 2024, Wang and Han， 2025]，本系统能够主动抑制非特异性
的泛化记忆（抑制中心节点连接），敏锐地捕捉并保留跨不同认知簇的远距离关联（横向思维/桥接边保留），并对高度重复的局部信息
产生习惯化反应（削弱冗余连接），其行为方式与人类大脑极为相似。
传统的查询依赖型GNN或PPR风格的扩展方法可以在图结构上进行多跳传播。但关键问题并不在于简单地扩大传播范围，而是在有限的top-
k预算下，保留查询相关证据信号相对于干扰噪声的优势。命题1(i)总结了这种信号-预算视角：软寻址可提升初始证据激活效果，结构化门
控机制在抑制噪声邻域的同时保留桥梁/证据路径，受控的实体到文档投影则将实体层面的优势转化为更高效的文档级检索。完整的定义、
假设和证明详见附录B。
目标图校准与跨图结构先验。人类记忆一方面会根据当前情境重新组织线索，另一方面又保留着相对稳定的结构化回忆习惯。在我们的自
演化图记忆模型中，每轮由写入器生成的图G都会改变局部拓扑结构和噪声分布，因此读取器无法仅依赖固定图中的传播模式。
由于作者不断改变记忆图，读者必须同时适应当前的目标图，并保留跨图的结构先验。这正是我们引入上下文-模式分解的原因。如命题1

的理论依据详见附录C和附录D。

读者训练。读者训练旨在通过两阶段流程学习跨图可迁移的检索偏差。首先，我们在多个增强的

作者-读者自进化。为应对第三个挑战，我们提出了一种自进化框架。每次自进化迭代包含两个阶
段：首先固定读者，利用其检索结果作为奖励来训练作者；随后使用更新后的作者生成新的图结
构，并继续训练读者。整个流程详见算法1。
命题1. SAGE的理论后果 
(i) 信号-预算效率。软寻址、结构化门控以及受控的实体到文档投影共同提升了证据信号
相对于干扰噪声的强度，从而降低了实现证据覆盖所需的前k个候选项预算。 
[信噪比证明] [预算证明] 
(ii) 上下文-结构分解。读者将可迁移的结构先验与目标图校准相结合，因此适应过程只需
修正图特有的残差即可。 
[证明] 
(iii) 进化稳定性。在有界图漂移条件下，连续的写入更新会引发文档评分的有界变化。 
[证明]
从理论角度来看，这一过程可被解释为近似的坐标优化。
关于联合记忆机制：写入器更新提升了图记忆的可读性，而读取器更新则减少了由写入器引起的
图分布偏移和奖励偏差。我们在附录F中提供了完整的坐标改进结果、代理奖励偏差上界以及单侧
更新瓶颈的分析。此外，命题1(iii)表明，尽管每次写入器更新都会改变自演化记忆中的图结构，
但读取器输出不会随图的演化而任意振荡。我们在附录J中提供了详细的训练、推理、内存以及选
择器-正则化器复杂度分析。
5 实验
本节围绕四个研究问题（RQs）展开实验评估。RQ1：SAGE是否能在多跳问答和开放域迁移等任
务中带来持续的收益；
RQ2：SAGE 是否是一种能够处理长期对话历史、知识更新和记忆幻觉的智能体记忆系统；
RQ3：作者-读者闭环是否真正带来了自我演进的益处；
RQ4：进一步分析特定设计在哪些方面以及如何带来性能提升。

5.3 进一步分析
如表4所示，SAGE展现出显著的速度优势，实现了最快的检索时间，表明其在实际应用和大
规模部署方面具有巨大潜力。为进一步分析SAGE的可解释性，我们对一个代表性案例的检索
子图进行了可视化展示，如图所示。详细案例研究见第1页。关于记忆写入器和读取器的详细
消融实验设计、分析及结果分别见附录H和附录G。
6 结论
我们提出了SAGE，一种自我演化的智能图记忆引擎，将记忆视为用于写作、阅读和持续优
化的动态基底。实验表明，SAGE在证据恢复、事实依据和检索效率方面均有提升，表明自
演化图记忆是构建长期语言智能体的有前景的基础。

内存写入器的附加分析
本附录对正文中的记忆写入实验进行了详细分析。
A.1 奖励设计与写作者行为
在表5中，不同的强化学习奖励会引导出不同的写入器行为。仅使用GFM预训练的模型在精确率/
召回率/可推导性上达到0.838/0.818/0.510，而经过微调的GFM模型则达到0.824/0.813/0.512，这表
明仅依赖监督式微调无法稳定提升冻结读取器对图记忆的利用效果。该结果也与我们的设定一致：
记忆写入器的目标并非复现静态的图格式。
RL-Recall 将精确率和召回率提升至 0.889/0.835，但可推导性下降至 0.502。这表明，仅奖励支持
性上下文覆盖会促使生成器存储更多本地相关证据，但并不必然形成完整的多跳推理链。RL-F1 
进一步将召回率提高到 0.881，但可推导性仅为 0.497，再次说明检索匹配质量与答案可推导性之
间存在差距：即使读者命中了支持性上下文，也不代表这些上下文的组织方式足以支撑答案推理。
相比之下，RL-Deduce 达到了 0.861/0.892/0.517 的表现，表明直接以答案可推导性作为反馈，能
促使生成器更关注实体间的桥梁、跨文档关系以及与答案相关的因果或属性路径。
RL-Hybrid 的精确率和召回率分别为 0.902 和 0.917，相比仅使用预训练模型分别提升了 0.064 和 0.
099，而 Deducible 达到 0.522。这表明混合奖励机制能够缓解单一奖励带来的偏差：它既避免了纯
召回奖励引入过多弱相关证据的问题，也防止了纯可推导性奖励过度偏好短路径或局部答案线索。
采用混合奖励加冻结答案 API 的方法实现了最高的 Deducible 值（0.526），但精确率和召回率下
降至 0.832 和 0.874。这说明更强的答案端反馈可以进一步提升推理的可用性，但也可能导致生成
器变得更加保守，只写出与最终答案直接相关的证据，从而牺牲了一部分支持性上下文的覆盖范
围。
A.2 跨域迁移
表6显示，基于HotpotQA/MuSiQue训练的基础生成器对新领域具有一定的迁移能力，但针对目标
领域的训练仍然至关重要。在GR-Bench上，Base→GRBench的精确率/召回率/可推导率为0.575/0.
609/0.411，而GR-Bench训练集→验证集则提升至0.794/0.833/0.596。这一提升表明，为多跳问答任
务所学习的写作策略能够迁移到结构化产品或领域图记忆任务中。
15

图4：在读者端固定敏感性分析。初始实体权重对奖励和可推导性的影响最为稳定；而top-k和排序器变
体则表现出非单调的预算与噪声权衡关系。
但目标领域中的实体类型、属性关系以及证据粒度仍需重新调整。
在HaluMem和LongMemEval上，跨域差异表现得更加明显。Base→HaluMem的结果为0.230/
0.448/0.299，在目标域训练后提升至0.312/0.708/0.438；Base→LongMemEval的结果为0.232/
0.376/0.475，在目标域训练后提升至0.377/0.439/0.531。这些结果表明，在智能体记忆任务中，
记忆写入不仅需要提取显式事实，还需保持用户偏好、时间顺序、状态更新以及长期一致性。
在传统的多跳问答任务中，支持上下文通常围绕单一问题形成相对静态的证据集合，而长期
记忆任务中的信息则随时间变化，并涉及个性化、冲突更新和上下文依赖性。因此，尽管面
向读者的强化学习反馈能够提供可迁移的写作原则，但来自目标域的交互反馈对于实现稳定
性能仍然至关重要。
A.3 写作规范与互动预算
表7显示，写作协议显著改变了精确率、召回率和可推导性之间的权衡关系。当Tight=True
时，结果为0.836/0.806/0.515；当Tight=False时，结果为0.845/0.851/0.506。在放宽协议后，
召回率明显提升，表明作者能够写出更多潜在相关的证据；然而，可推导性有所下降，说明
新增的证据中也包含更多噪声、冗余事实或关联较弱的局部信息。尽管这些内容可能扩大了
支持上下文的覆盖范围，但却会稀释真正支撑答案的推理链条。
迭代写作进一步凸显了交互预算的作用。在迭代模式下，12轮交互时，精确率/召回率/可推
导性分别为0.852/0.829/0.516；当增加至20轮时，召回率达到最高值0.881，表明多轮读者反
馈有助于作者完成跨文档的桥梁路径构建。而在24轮、宽松标准下，精确率和可推导性分别
达到0.863和0.531，但召回率却回落至0.826。这说明更多的交互轮次并非“越多越好”：其
益处源于作者根据读者反馈对图结构进行修订；而当协议过于宽松或写作空间过大时，新增
内容可能改变读者的排序，导致部分黄金支持上下文被挤出前几名结果。
A.4 读者端灵敏度
图4分析了冻结读者设置对写者训练结果的影响。首先，top-k预算的扫描结果显示了一种非
单调趋势：当k=5时，奖励值和可推导性分别为0.623/0.528，是该组中的较优设置；当k=40
时，奖励值保持在0.622，但可推导性下降至0.518；当k=60时，奖励值进一步降至0.591。这
表明，扩大检索预算并不一定带来更好的读者反馈。尽管更大的top-k能提升潜在覆盖范围，
但也引入了更多弱相关或冗余的证据，从而稀释了真正支持答案的推理链条。
16

图5：训练正则化与缩放分析。在这一组实验结果中，较大的展开组大小和适中的预热比例显示出轻微
优势，但其提升效果小于奖励设计、读者端初始实体权重以及写作协议所带来的影响。
排序器的各个变体也体现了类似的覆盖范围与噪声之间的权衡。topk20 在奖励值和可推导性方
面表现最佳，分别为 0.630 和 0.544；id f-only 和 raw 的奖励值均为 0.626，但其可推导性得分
分别为 0.538 和 0.519；而 idf-topk60 则下降至 0.595/0.506。这表明，阅读端排序器不能仅依赖
实体重叠或扩大候选集，而必须在实体匹配、语义相关性和上下文紧凑性之间取得平衡。对于
写作端而言，排序器过于弱化会导致难以有效读取图结构，而候选空间过于宽泛则会放大噪声
写入的负面影响。
初始实体权重是读者端三组设置中最稳定的因素。当启用初始实体权重时，奖励值达到0.639，
可推导性为0.525；而禁用后，奖励值显著下降至0.547，可推导性降至0.505。即使在禁用后增
加预算，off@10和off@40的奖励值也仅恢复到0.613和0.615。这表明，在多跳图检索中，初始
实体锚点至关重要：它帮助读者从问题实体出发进入正确的局部子图，并沿着作者编写的桥接
关系扩展至支持答案的证据。若缺少这一锚点，单纯增加检索预算也无法完全弥补图遍历方向
上的偏差。
A.5 训练稳定性与正则化
图5展示了训练正则化和展开设置对生成器的影响。首先，重复惩罚同时影响奖励值和可推导
性，但其趋势并非单调。在无任何惩罚时，结果为0.585/0.510；当惩罚设为0.10时，提升至0.
610/0.520；惩罚设为0.50时，达到该组最佳结果0.619/0.522；而进一步将惩罚提高到1.00时，
结果下降至0.597/0.500。这表明，适度的三元组重复惩罚有助于抑制冗余边和循环表达，但过
强的惩罚可能会限制生成器对关键事实的必要重复。尤其是在多跳推理中，同一个桥接实体往
往需要出现在多个关系路径中，因此重复并不总是无意义的噪声。
 rollout过滤带来了稳定但有限的提升。当过滤功能关闭时，奖励值/可推断性为0.585/0.510；应
用thr_80、thr_90和thr_95后，奖励值分别提升至0.621、0.617和0.625，而可推断性则稳定在0.
516–0.518之间。这表明，剔除低质量的rollout可以减少噪声轨迹对策略更新的干扰，使模型更
稳定地学习有效的写作策略。然而，不同阈值之间的差异较小，说明过滤的主要作用是去除明
显负面的样本，而非决定最终性能的上限。
17

批量大小和预热比例进一步影响训练的稳定性。当批量大小从 n=1 增加到 n=10 时，奖励值/可推
导性从 0.610/0.511 上升至 0.620/0.531，表明更大的批量大小能够提供更可靠的相对偏好估计，有
助于强化学习更准确地区分有效与无效的写作内容。最佳预热比约为 0.20，此时可推导性达到 0.
529；预热过少可能导致早期策略更新不稳定，而预热过多则可能延迟强化学习信号的作用。总
体而言，这些正则化和训练规模设置虽能提升稳定性，但其带来的增益小于奖励设计、读者端初
始实体权重以及交互协议本身所带来的改进。这表明，记忆写入器的核心提升源于面向读者的强
化学习反馈：它迫使图构建器学会保留支持答案推导的桥梁实体、跨文档关系和证据链，同时减
少重复结构和无关的局部事实。

G 内存写入器分析
本节在保持记忆读取器不变的情况下，进一步分析了记忆写入器。实验主要使用HotpotQA和MuSiQue
数据集。为了进一步考察领域迁移能力，我们还在GRBench-Amazon、HaluMem-Medium和
LongMemEval-Oracle[?？]上评估了训练好的写入策略。我们主要报告精确率（Precision）、召回率
（Recall）和可推导性（Deducible）：前两项衡量读取器检索出的文本是否覆盖了标准支持上下文，
而可推导性由判断者决定，即标准答案能否从检索到的上下文中推断出来，因此更能直接反映图记忆
是否可用于推理。
表5显示，不同的奖励机制具有不同的偏好。总体而言，RL-Hybrid取得了最佳的整体效果，表明混合
奖励能够同时约束图谱写作的选择性和覆盖性。Hybrid + frozen answer API在可推导性方面表现最高，
但检索的精确率/召回率略低，说明答案端反馈有助于提升推理可用性，但也可能导致写作者更倾向
于保守地生成直接支持答案的证据。表6表明，在HotpotQA/MuSiQue上训练的写作者可以迁移到
GRBench、HaluMem和LongMemEval任务中，但在目标领域继续训练仍能带来显著提升。这说明智能
体记忆场景中的记忆结构与传统多跳问答并不完全相同，因此目标领域的反馈仍然至关重要。表7进
一步表明，写作协议和交互预算会影响覆盖性与噪声之间的权衡。放宽严格的提示可以提高召回率，
但会降低可推导性；增加迭代轮次有助于完成跨文档的桥接路径，但当预算过大或协议过于宽松时，
可能产生负面影响。更详细的读者端敏感性、训练稳定性以及正则化分析详见附录A。

表8总结了实验结果。第一组评估读者如何处理碎片化的线索。移除结构化查询规划、别名/
约束线索或全局软寻址，迫使读者更多地依赖表面级的查询匹配或少量锚定实体，从而直接
检验当远程桥接节点未被初始激活时，完整的证据链是否仍能被恢复。第二组研究读者是否
以一种学习性和选择性的方式利用图结构。移除结构门控或将其替换为均匀的消息传递，用
于测试将中心节点边、桥接边、冗余边和噪声捷径同等对待是否会损害检索效果。第三组考
察上下文-模式分解：模式通道捕捉可迁移的结构化阅读模式，而上下文通道则使读者适应当
前作者生成的图结构。最后一组评估选择器、实体到文档的投影、GFM预训练以及监督式检
索微调的效果。
消融实验结果展示了各个记忆-阅读组件的相对贡献。首先，结构化查询规划和全局软寻址对
于碎片化线索检索至关重要：移除这些模块会明显削弱性能，尤其在MuSiQue和
2WikiMultiHopQA数据集上表现更为显著，因为这些任务中的证据链更可能依赖隐式的桥接
实体。其次，结构化门控传播相较于均匀的消息传递具有持续性的性能提升，表明应选择性
地利用图结构，而非将其作为固定的扩展规则。在各类结构输入中，图级摘要的影响相对较
小，而节点级和边对特征在识别枢纽节点、桥接节点以及跨社区的证据路径方面更为重要。
第三，模式先验和上下文校准通道均对性能有所贡献，说明阅读器在适应当前写者生成的图
结构的同时，也能从保留可迁移的结构先验中获益。最后，监督检索微调对于使GFM阅读器
与文档级证据检索对齐至关重要，而GFM预训练则提供了可迁移的结构化初始化，从而提升
了模型在不同数据集上的稳定性。
